danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites positioned throughout destructible block maze
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring elimination via bomb placement; visual markers of dangerous zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinctive sprite at conclusion of each world; transitions to giant machine form after defeat on foot
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger levels; boss first appears as defeatable enemy, then retreats into armored form signaling phase transition

danger_signals.visual_form: Maze-filling block structures that require bomb detonation to clear
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard and resource gate; blocks conceal power-ups and switches needed to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Interactive elements within maze that must be flipped to open gates
danger_signals.meaning: Progression requirement; visual indication of mandatory tasks before area exit becomes accessible

safety_signals.visual_form: Exit point that becomes accessible after all monsters defeated and switches flipped
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage forward; visual confirmation of objective completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectibles revealed under destroyed blocks (bomb count increase, firepower boost, speed enhancement, special abilities)
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment; visual indicator of capability enhancement and tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Open areas between destructible blocks after player demolition
safety_signals.meaning: Safe movement corridors; reduced threat density in cleared zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bomberman sprite identity established through series convention
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player occupies prison cell in alien space station dungeon (story context); fights through five worlds each associated with distinct boss identity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Named identities: Magnet Bomber, Golem Bomber, Pretty Bomber, Brain Bomber, Plasma Bomber (leader); each presumably has distinct visual appearance
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each boss transitions from on-foot combat form to giant machine form, signaling power escalation and phase change
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Space station setting establishes alien/technological faction aesthetic; dungeon progression through five worlds corresponds to five boss encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bomberman placed in dungeon cell of space station; confined environment establishes stakes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must fight through maze areas to escape; progression through five worlds represents liberation path

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active invasion/occupation (Five Dastardly Bombers control Earth)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each of five worlds visually distinct by associated boss entity and environmental aesthetic; progression represents reclamation of territories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory defeat of all monsters and switch activation in each world before gate access; reflects need to clear occupation forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite at world conclusion; escalation to giant machine form indicates consolidated power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Two-phase combat: first on-foot, then mechanized; visual transformation signals boss desperation and final threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Open gate to next world becomes accessible; visual reward for objective completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player transitions to next world, encountering new boss and terrain, continuing liberation campaign

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Golden visual effect applied to match winner; gold coloration signals tournament progression status
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Winner carries momentum item into subsequent matches; visual distinction enables rapid identification of advantage holder

environmental_storytelling.example: Story mode places Bomberman in alien space station dungeon; player physically traverses through five distinct worlds corresponding to five boss encounters, visually communicating liberation journey and increasing threat escalation
Each world tied to named Dastardly Bomber identity (Magnet Bomber first, Plasma Bomber final), presumably with distinct visual/environmental aesthetic signaling different territorial domains
Destructible blocks, monsters, and switch requirements communicate need to clear/prepare areas; visual obstruction represents occupation/danger requiring player action
Gate accessibility tied to monster elimination and switch activation; visual barrier communicates progression requirement and objective clarity
Destroyed blocks reveal capability upgrades (bomb count, firepower, speed, special abilities); visual discovery mechanism communicates player empowerment and reward for exploration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears on foot in maze-like area; visual presentation as defeatable opponent
Retreats into giant machine form; visual escalation signals desperation and phase transition
Area clears; gate opens visually; progression enabled
Positioned as static/patrol threats; require bomb elimination to clear area
Visually present within maze; gate remains closed (visual barrier persists)
All switches activated together enable gate opening; visual reward mechanism
Golden visual effect applied; wheel spin determines next-match power-up advantage; visual distinction signals momentum advantage

notes: Super Bomberman 2 uses minimal narrative complexity for a 1994 SNES title; world-state communication relies heavily on visual progression through dungeon structure and boss encounters. The alien occupation narrative provides contextual framing but is conveyed primarily through story text rather than environmental visualization. Boss design with dual-form system (on-foot → giant machine) represents primary visual danger escalation mechanic. Multiplayer mode employs golden coloration to mark tournament progression state. Power-up system uses block destruction as visual discovery mechanism. The game prioritizes functional visual clarity (maze navigation, threat identification, objective markers) over narrative environmental detail. Faction distinction relies on named boss identities rather than documented visual/color differentiation in available sources.
